title: microlaena tasmanica
intro: Microlaena tasmanica, also known as Tasmanian woody rice-flower, is a flowering plant native to Tasmania and southeastern Australia. It is a type of grass with medicinal properties, traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine to treat various health issues. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin irritations, while also promoting wound healing and immune system function. In herbal preparations, Microlaena tasmanica is often combined with other plants to create teas, infusions, and topical creams that can be used to support overall health and well-being.
